sidewalk.notes: Minimum clear walking zone width; PROWAG R302.3 requires 4 ft absolute minimum

planting-strip.notes: Buffer between pedestrian zone and roadway; provides shade, stormwater management

furniture-zone.notes: Zone for street furniture, signs, utilities; must not encroach on pedestrian access route

bike-lane.notes: Conventional bike lane adjacent to travel lane or parking

bike-lane-protected.notes: Physically separated cycle track; 7 ft preferred for two-way, 5 ft minimum one-way

buffer.notes: Physical separation between bike lane and travel/parking lane; may include vertical elements

parking-lane.notes: Parallel parking lane width; 8 ft standard, 7 ft acceptable in constrained corridors

travel-lane.notes: General travel lane; 10 ft preferred on urban streets to reduce speeds; 12 ft max for arterials

turn-lane.notes: Dedicated turn lane; same width guidance as travel lanes

transit-lane.source: NACTO Transit Street Design Guide p.62
transit-lane.notes: Dedicated bus/transit lane; 11 ft minimum for standard bus, 12 ft recommended

median.notes: Raised or painted median; 6 ft minimum for pedestrian refuge island

curb.notes: Standard curb width; curb reveal height typically 6 in per AASHTO
